description: Vetting policy — single source of truth for what agents may and may not run. This file replaces hardcoded patterns in dotfiles-guard.py and opencode.json.

signing.todo: migrate to YubiKey-resident key — FIDO2 PIN blocked (needs physical reinsertion), PIV PIN unknown (3 tries remain). User declined FIDO reset (passkeys in use). Migration requires PIN knowledge or new YubiKey.

token_audit.scopes: admin:public_key, admin:ssh_signing_key, gist, read:org, repo, workflow
token_audit.storage: ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (90 bytes, 0400, readable by agent as this user)
token_audit.admin_ssh_signing_key: CRITICAL: agent can register new signing keys to the account without user intervention. This bypasses ALL signing enforcement.
token_audit.repo: Full write access to all repos the user can access.
token_audit.recommendation: Replace with fine-grained token scoped to eqdmc/security and dotfiles repos only, WITHOUT admin:ssh_signing_key.
token_audit.todo: User must create a new fine-grained token at https://github.com/settings/tokens with minimal scopes (repo: eqdmc/security + eqdmc/dotfiles, no admin:ssh_signing_key). Then replace ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml. Keep old token until new one is verified.

criteria.description: Recent releases, active dev, issue response
CVE history, audit trail, vulnerability reporting
How well features match the use case
Integration with our stack (Fedora, btrfs, systemd)

containment.description: This system prevents honest-mistake installs and provides an audit trail. It does NOT contain a compromised agent, because the agent holds the credentials (gh token with repo+admin:ssh_signing_key scopes) and exec capability that would be needed to forge approvals.
containment.why: Session started from /home/z, which has no opencode.json. Resolved config has no permission block. Repo-level opencode.json deny rules are NOT loaded. Every command runs without restriction.
real_containment_requires: Fine-grained token scoped to minimum repos, without admin:ssh_signing_key
Network egress control or sandboxed exec environment
opencode.json permission block in the global config or session start directory
